name: Bayfront Park & Marina
address: 5 Bayfront Dr, Sarasota
blurb: Downtown's front porch on the bay — sailboats, a splash fountain, and the best sunset photo in the city framed by the Ringling Bridge.
tip: Walk the bridge at golden hour — the unofficial test-drive for downtown condo life.

name: Lido Key Beach
address: 400 Ben Franklin Dr, Lido Key
blurb: Quieter and more polished than Siesta, a short stroll from St. Armands Circle — the easiest beach day if you want lunch and shopping in the same trip.
tip: Buyers love this stretch for walkable beach plus dining without Siesta's traffic.

name: Longboat Key — North End
address: Longboat Key
blurb: Eleven miles of low-key, manicured island living — no neon, no crowds, just resorts, golf, and some of the most exclusive Gulf-front addresses on the coast.
tip: This is the move-up market. Inventory is thin and prices reward patience.

name: Marie Selby Botanical Gardens
address: 1534 Mound St, Sarasota
blurb: Bayfront gardens famous for orchids and epiphytes, plus a banyan grove and a tree-canopy walk. A green exhale minutes from downtown.
tip: The downtown campus and the Spanish Point campus to the south are both worth it.

name: Mote Marine Aquarium
address: 1600 Ken Thompson Pkwy, City Island
blurb: Sharks, manatees, and sea-turtle research on City Island — a genuine working marine lab, not just a tourist tank.
tip: A new mainland campus is coming near I-75; the current City Island site's future is fun to speculate about.

name: Sarasota Farmers Market
address: State St & Lemon Ave, Downtown
blurb: 70+ vendors take over downtown every Saturday morning — produce, flowers, prepared food, and live music. The week's best free morning out.
tip: Saturdays 7am–1pm. Grab a coffee, do a loop, then walk Main Street while it's quiet.

name: Sarasota Opera House
address: 61 N Pineapple Ave, Sarasota
blurb: A restored 1926 Mediterranean Revival gem anchoring the downtown arts district — a big part of why Sarasota punches above its weight culturally.
tip: The arts calendar is a genuine value driver downtown; it's the first thing relocating buyers fall for.

name: Siesta Key Beach
address: 948 Beach Rd, Siesta Key
blurb: The headliner — 99% quartz sand that stays cool underfoot even at midday, with a gentle Gulf shelf that makes it family-safe. Consistently ranked among the top beaches in the country.
tip: Park at Beach Access 5 before 9am on weekends, or skip the lot and bike in from the Village. The Sunday-evening drum circle on the south end is the real local ritual.

name: South Lido Nature Park
address: 2201 Ben Franklin Dr, Lido Key
blurb: Mangrove tunnels you can kayak, a quiet Gulf beach, and shade under Australian pines — the wild, unpolished side of Lido.
tip: Rent a kayak and paddle the mangrove maze at high tide.

name: St. Armands Circle
address: St. Armands Circle, Sarasota
blurb: An open-air ring of boutiques, galleries, and restaurants between downtown and Lido Beach. Equal parts shopping trip and people-watching.
tip: Living within a golf-cart ride of the Circle is its own micro-market — ask me about it.

name: The Ringling
address: 5401 Bay Shore Rd, Sarasota
blurb: John Ringling's 66-acre bayfront estate: a world-class art museum, the Cà d'Zan mansion, and a circus museum. The single best rainy-day plan in town.
tip: Mondays the art galleries are free. Give yourself half a day — people always underestimate it.

name: Van Wezel Performing Arts Hall
address: 777 N Tamiami Trl, Sarasota
blurb: The unmistakable purple seashell on the bayfront — Broadway tours, concerts, and comedy with the bay shimmering through the lobby glass.
tip: The surrounding bayfront is slated for major redevelopment — a long-game reason to watch nearby addresses.

address: 700 Benjamin Franklin Dr, Sarasota, FL 34236, USA
blurb:
At Drift Kitchen & Bar you'll find elevated Gulf-view dining perched on the 8th floor of Lido Beach Resort, offering panoramic, floor-to-ceiling views over Lido Beach and Sarasota's coastline. The menu highlights fresh seafood, house-made pastas, brick-oven pizzas, and contemporary American dishes that blend coastal flavors with a refined, resort-style touch. But in the end, you're here for the views and the resort feel.

Open to the public

name: Siesta Promenade (New Benderson Dev - 2025?)
blurb:
Estimated 2025 for retail and apartments, then hotel after.The project will comprise a total of 10 retail and restaurant buildings, including a yet-to-be-named grocer. It will have 140,000 square feet of retail space, with a minimum of 7,000 square feet of office space, and an apartment building with 414 units, 25 of which will be affordable. Siesta Promenade will also include an 80-foot high, 130-room hotel, which will be larger than any single hotel on nearby Siesta Key, where space for such a dense hotel is limited.
https://www.sarasotamagazine.com/home-and-real-estate/2023/09/benderson-siesta-promenade-south-sarasota-development

name: SOTA Hotel & Residences (FUTURE)
address: 1703 Main St, Sarasota, FL 34236, USA
blurb:
Breaking ground summer 2023SOTA will be 16 stories high and have luxury residences, hotel suites and a 4,500-square-foot ground-level restaurant open to the public.
SOTA will have 35 condominium residences spanning six floors, 120 hotel rooms managed by Tapestry Collection by Hilton, and a 4,500-square-foot restaurant on the ground floor.
Residential condos at SOTA range from $1.7 million to $5 million. For comparison, nearby luxury condo buildings, like The Edge, which is scheduled to break ground by April, start at $2.6 million, while condos at the newly finished The Collection, start at $2 million-and only one unit remains available.

blurb:
Full Membership (all the same):
- $20,000 one time
- $9,400 /yr (couples)
- $1,500/yr Food & Bev minimumSlip Rates (Currently waitlisted):
- $13.25/ft in water or $18.75/ft on lift
- 45' in water slips and 14,000 lb lift caps-Social club (dining Tues-Sun ala carte, brunch on the weekends), Bocce, Tennis, Pool
Our Club's reconstructed 42-slip marina offers 21 boat lifts from 10,000-14,000-pound capacity, 21 in-water slips that can accommodate sail and power vessels from 28 to 50 feet, and a kayak lift. The marina also offers diesel fuel and gasoline, along with complete pump-out facilities. Complimentary Wi-Fi is available at all slips. The marina is staffed full-time by experienced team members prepared to meet all members' boating needs. Marina staff monitors VHF channel 16.
